problem_statement: The 'other' bucket (16% of volume) is inflated because agents fail to recognise contract template questions written in HR jargon, meaning tickets that belong in a known category go unsorted and sit idle.
ai_solution_concept: An LLM can extract intent from domain-specific HR language across multiple languages and map it to the correct category, reducing 'other' bucket misclassification.

sub_scores.name: volume
sub_scores.rationale: The 'other' bucket represents 16% of ~2,100 weekly tickets, meaning roughly 336 tickets per week are affected. This is a meaningful slice of volume (10–25% range) consistent with a Material score.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: our "other" bucket is about 16% of volume. Half of those are actually contract template questions that the agents don't recognise

sub_scores.name: hours_saved
sub_scores.rationale: With ~168 misclassified contract-template tickets per week sitting idle, and triage times ranging from 90 seconds to 5–6 minutes, the aggregate agent time wasted on re-routing and idle queue time is in the tens of agent-hours per week range, consistent with a Material score.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: Clean ticket, 90 seconds. Messy one — long German email from an HR generalist who's described three problems in one message, with a screenshot of their payroll preview — easily five, six minutes before we start actually helping.

sub_scores.name: customer_friction
sub_scores.rationale: Tickets sitting idle in the 'other' bucket create delays and missed SLAs, which is a recurring source of customer frustration, though no explicit churn or NPS mention is made for this specific sub-problem.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: tickets that belong in a known category go unsorted and sit idle

sub_scores.name: data_availability
sub_scores.rationale: Tickets already flow through Zendesk with subject lines and body text available. The nine category labels are well-defined and historical categorisation data exists, making this a straightforward classification task with inputs already in usable form.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: A ticket comes in through Zendesk — email, in-product help widget, or escalated out of Fin on the help-centre. Agent reads the subject, opens it, and tries to bucket it. We run nine categories

sub_scores.name: llm_capability
sub_scores.rationale: Extracting intent from domain-specific HR jargon (e.g., 'Arbeitsvertrag') and mapping it to one of nine known categories is a textbook LLM classification task. Multi-language support is well within current LLM capability.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: the customer described them in HR-speak — "the seniority field isn't pulling through to my Arbeitsvertrag."

sub_scores.name: process_stability
sub_scores.rationale: The nine-category taxonomy is documented and stable, and the Zendesk workflow is well-described and consistent. The categorisation process itself is not in flux, making integration straightforward.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: We run nine categories: payroll, time & attendance, absence, contracts & documents, integrations, mobile app, GDPR / data subject requests, account & permissions, and "other."

sub_scores.name: dev_effort
sub_scores.rationale: This is primarily an integration task — connecting Zendesk ticket intake to an LLM classifier with a fixed nine-category schema. No new data infrastructure is needed, pointing to a one-to-two week build.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: A ticket comes in through Zendesk — email, in-product help widget, or escalated out of Fin on the help-centre.

sub_scores.name: api_cost
sub_scores.rationale: At ~2,100 tickets per week (~9,000/month), classifying short-to-medium ticket texts with a lightweight LLM call per ticket would cost well under $200/month at current API pricing, consistent with the 'Cheap' anchor.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: About 2,100 tickets a week into Tier 1. Sixty percent German, thirty English, the rest split across French, Spanish, Italian, Dutch.

sub_scores.name: maintenance_burden
sub_scores.rationale: The nine-category taxonomy is stable and the classification prompt is unlikely to need frequent updates. Occasional prompt tweaks if new product areas emerge, but no dedicated ops function required.
sub_scores.evidence_quote: We run nine categories: payroll, time & attendance, absence, contracts & documents, integrations, mobile app, GDPR / data subject requests, account & permissions, and "other."
